ddls:
CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region */,
  "region_code" TEXT /* Code for the region */,
  "geographical_area" REAL /* Geographical area of the region */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_types" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the product type (e.g., conventional, organic) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product type */,
  "unit_of_measure" TEXT /* Unit of measure for the product type (e.g., kg, lbs) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market data entry */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date of the market data */,
  "average_price" REAL /* Average price of the product */,
  "total_volume" REAL /* Total volume of the product sold */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* ID of the region where the market data was collected */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year the market data was collected */,
  "product_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product type */,
  "market_trend" TEXT /* Market trend (e.g., increasing, decreasing, stable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_product_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_type_id") REFERENCES product_types ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_market_trend FOREIGN KEY ("market_trend") REFERENCES market_trends ("trend_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_market_data_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sales_data" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sales data entry */,
  "market_data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the market data the sales data belongs to */,
  "product_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product type the sales data belongs to */,
  "small_bags" REAL /* Number of small bags sold */,
  "large_bags" REAL /* Number of large bags sold */,
  "xlarge_bags" REAL /* Number of xlarge bags sold */,
  "total_sales" REAL /* Total sales of the product type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_market_data_id FOREIGN KEY ("market_data_id") REFERENCES market_data ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sales_data_product_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_type_id") REFERENCES product_types ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user's role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role the permission belongs to */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_permissions_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "market_trends" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each market trend */,
  "trend_name" TEXT /* Name of the market trend */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the market trend */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role relationship */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("id")
)
